summary_lines:
case_id=100; prompt=What is the color of the woman's handbag?
(A) white
(B) red
(C) brown
(D) black

Choices:
A. white
B. red
C. brown
D. black; expected=C; answer=D. black

case_id=4; prompt=What is the pose of the woman with yellow backpack?
(A) walking
(B) running
(C) squatting
(D) standing

Choices:
A. walking
B. running
C. squatting
D. standing; expected=C; answer=D. standing

family_toolbox.name: generic_visual_focus
family_toolbox.applicability_conditions: Creates a layout-preserving focus artifact for left/right comparison by emphasizing visually salient regions and candidate object extents in natural images without relying on object-specific detectors. Robustly converts PIL images, numpy arrays, grayscale, RGBA, and unusual intermediate values into concrete numpy image arrays before OpenCV operations, and always saves a real artifact under artifacts/ for valid inputs.
notes: Creates a layout-preserving focus artifact for left/right comparison by emphasizing visually salient regions and candidate object extents in natural images without relying on object-specific detectors. Robustly converts PIL images, numpy arrays, grayscale, RGBA, and unusual intermediate values into concrete numpy image arrays before OpenCV operations, and always saves a real artifact under artifacts/ for valid inputs.

family_toolbox.name: localized_color_focus
family_toolbox.applicability_conditions: Creates a concrete artifact image under artifacts/ showing a region-proposal overlay plus a candidate zoom strip for localized inspection of colorful or high-detail regions. Useful for direct visible-color questions about clothing, accessories, or other salient local objects without assuming a fixed class or fixed position.
notes: Creates a concrete artifact image under artifacts/ showing a region-proposal overlay plus a candidate zoom strip for localized inspection of colorful or high-detail regions. Useful for direct visible-color questions about clothing, accessories, or other salient local objects without assuming a fixed class or fixed position.

common_failure_signals: when the question depends on reading text, numbers, or labels
when the target object is extremely obvious and occupies a large distinctive region, making localization unnecessary
recommended_trigger_conditions: when the question asks for the color of a named object, accessory, or clothing item visible in the image
when the target item is likely small or attached to a person and global inspection may miss its true color
when success depends on isolating one referenced region before judging color
negative_trigger_conditions: when the question depends on reading text, numbers, or labels
when the target object is extremely obvious and occupies a large distinctive region, making localization unnecessary
when the asked attribute is not a visible local attribute

notes: This is the core reusable family strategy: first localize the referenced object without hardcoding object type or position, then inspect only that region for color. It directly addresses the observed failure mode of skipping localized visual inspection.

The family-level primitive is localized color focus. Using it first can quickly emphasize candidate local color regions, after which a region attribute check confirms the color. This is efficient for direct visible color questions and generalizes across many object categories.
A no-tool path should remain available for easy direct-attribute cases where the object's color is obvious. This avoids unnecessary tool use while still restricting it to low-ambiguity situations.

This is the core reusable family strategy: first localize the referenced object without hardcoding object type or position, then inspect only that region for color. It directly addresses the observed failure mode of skipping localized visual inspection.

family_toolbox.name: generic_visual_focus
family_toolbox.applicability_conditions: Creates a layout-preserving focus artifact for left/right comparison by emphasizing visually salient regions and candidate object extents in natural images without relying on object-specific detectors. Robustly converts PIL images, numpy arrays, grayscale, RGBA, and unusual intermediate values into concrete numpy image arrays before OpenCV operations, and always saves a real artifact under artifacts/ for valid inputs.
notes: Creates a layout-preserving focus artifact for left/right comparison by emphasizing visually salient regions and candidate object extents in natural images without relying on object-specific detectors. Robustly converts PIL images, numpy arrays, grayscale, RGBA, and unusual intermediate values into concrete numpy image arrays before OpenCV operations, and always saves a real artifact under artifacts/ for valid inputs.

family_toolbox.name: localized_color_focus
family_toolbox.applicability_conditions: Creates a concrete artifact image under artifacts/ showing a region-proposal overlay plus a candidate zoom strip for localized inspection of colorful or high-detail regions. Useful for direct visible-color questions about clothing, accessories, or other salient local objects without assuming a fixed class or fixed position.
notes: Creates a concrete artifact image under artifacts/ showing a region-proposal overlay plus a candidate zoom strip for localized inspection of colorful or high-detail regions. Useful for direct visible-color questions about clothing, accessories, or other salient local objects without assuming a fixed class or fixed position.

common_failure_signals: when the two queried objects are already straightforward to localize without scene-level context
when the scene is cluttered enough that caption-style description is unlikely to resolve referents
recommended_trigger_conditions: when object names may be broad, unusual, or visually confusable and a quick scene summary can disambiguate them
when the image contains multiple candidate instances and extra context helps identify the intended pair
when a natural-image left/right comparison still reduces to localized horizontal ordering after context gathering
negative_trigger_conditions: when the two queried objects are already straightforward to localize without scene-level context
when the scene is cluttered enough that caption-style description is unlikely to resolve referents
when the task is not a simple left/right comparison between visible objects

notes: This directly matches the family primitive: localize the two queried objects, then use a position-focused annotation step to compare their x-locations. It is reusable across object categories and addresses the recurring no-tool-used failures.

A brief global description can help determine what objects are present before localization, reducing misgrounding for generic relative-position questions. After that, the same reusable horizontal comparison primitive applies.

For clear cases, a dedicated spatial-position aid may be enough to support reliable left/right reasoning while keeping the chain compact. This offers a lower-cost option within the same family-level pattern.

Some family instances are trivial enough for direct reasoning, so preserving a no-tool path is useful. However, because the observed failures came from not using tools, this should be reserved for only the clearest examples.

A brief global description can help determine what objects are present before localization, reducing misgrounding for generic relative-position questions. After that, the same reusable horizontal comparison primitive applies.
